task_completion.description: Navigate to the CAMEO Chemicals website.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A web browser is open to https://cameochemicals.noaa.gov/ or shows search results within the CAMEO database.

task_completion.description: Create the required safety report file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or file manager shows a file named 'confined_space_vapor_report.txt' located in the '~/Documents' (or /home/ga/Documents) directory.

task_completion.description: Include the required headers in the report.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text file contains the exact headers 'CONFINED SPACE VAPOR DENSITY ASSESSMENT' and 'SUMMARY' with the '========================================' separators.

task_completion.description: Record the vapor density values for all 5 chemicals.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text file lists Hydrogen Sulfide, Ammonia, Chlorine, Carbon Monoxide, and Sulfur Dioxide, each with a 'Vapor Density' value matching the CAMEO database (e.g., ~1.19, ~0.60, ~2.49, ~0.97, ~2.26).

task_completion.description: Classify each chemical's vapor density relative to air.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Each chemical in the text file has a 'Classification' of HEAVIER, LIGHTER, or SIMILAR (Hydrogen Sulfide: HEAVIER, Ammonia: LIGHTER, Chlorine: HEAVIER, Carbon Monoxide: SIMILAR, Sulfur Dioxide: HEAVIER).

task_completion.description: List the chemicals that accumulate in LOW areas in the summary.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'SUMMARY' section at the bottom of the file lists the HEAVIER chemicals (Hydrogen Sulfide, Chlorine, Sulfur Dioxide) as accumulating in LOW areas.

integrity.description: The agent actively searched the CAMEO Chemicals database.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent typing chemical names into the CAMEO search bar and viewing the chemical data pages, rather than hallucinating the data.

integrity.description: The agent created the report using standard tools rather than a single terminal echo command bypassing GUI interaction.
integrity.visual_evidence: A text editor (like gedit, nano, or mousepad) is visible in the screenshots being used to format and save the report.

integrity.description: The agent did not simply copy-paste a pre-written response.
integrity.visual_evidence: The report contains actual numerical values for Vapor Density retrieved from the web, which were not provided in the prompt.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Output file: /home/ga/Documents/confined_space_vapor_report.txt. Expected Vapor Density values and classifications: Hydrogen Sulfide: ~1.19 (HEAVIER); Ammonia: ~0.60 (LIGHTER); Chlorine: ~2.49 (HEAVIER); Carbon Monoxide: ~0.97 (SIMILAR); Sulfur Dioxide: ~2.26 (HEAVIER). The SUMMARY section for 'LOW areas' must list the HEAVIER chemicals: Hydrogen Sulfide, Chlorine, and Sulfur Dioxide.
